big_picture: Oil prices surged amid escalating conflict in West Asia, with the Iranian Navy guiding an Indian tanker through the Hormuz strait. The Centre is working on strategies to minimise the West Asia crisis' impact, as stated during the Parliament Budget Session. In Kerala polls, a BJP candidate faced trouble with election officials over ‘Hindu MLA’ remarks. The Kerala High Court stayed a judgment removing Vellappally Natesan as General Secretary of SNDP Yogam.

The Bharatiya Gorkha Prajatantrik Morcha is also involved in political developments in West Bengal, where the Trinamool Congress is preparing for the 2026 Assembly elections.

The role demands expertise in go-to-market strategies, regulatory navigation, and closing utility-scale deals, targeting products such as the Megapack for grid projects and Powerwall for residential use.

articles.title: RBI approves the voluntary amalgamation of Lalbaug Co-operative Bank Ltd., Vadodara, Gujarat with Akhand Anand Co-operative Bank Ltd., Surat, Gujarat
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India, in exercise of the powers conferred under sub-section (4) of Section 44A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of Lalbaug Co-operative Bank Ltd., Vadodara, Gujarat with Akhand Anand Co-operative Bank Ltd., Surat, Gujarat. The Scheme will come into effect from March 23, 2026 (Monday). All the branches of Lalbaug Co-operative Bank Ltd., Vadodara, Gujarat will function as branches of Akhand Anand Co-operative B

articles.title: 'Nothing Left To Decide' : Supreme Court Disposes Plea Challenging Sonam Wangchuk's Detention Noting His Release
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a habeas corpus petition filed by Dr Gitanjali Angmo challenging the detention of her husband, Ladakh activist Sonam Wangchuk, under the National Security Act (NSA). The Union Government had already revoked Wangchuk's detention on March 14, making the petition infructuous. The bench noted there was nothing left to decide. The case highlights concerns over detention laws and civil liberties in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court today disposed of the� habeas corpus petition filed by Dr Gitanjali Angmo challenging the detention of Ladakh activist and her husband Sonam Wangchuk's detention under the National Security Act(NSA) as illegal. A bench comprising Justice Aravind Kumar and Justice PB Varale noted that the Union Government on March 14 revoked the detention and the petition therefore has become infructuous. The bench observed that there was nothing left in the matter to decide. The Centre revoked

articles.title: 'Shocking' : Supreme Court Slams Haryana Police For Insensitive Probe Into Rape Of 3-Year-Old Girl
articles.summary: The Supreme Court sharply criticized the Haryana Police for their insensitive investigation into the rape of a 3-year-old girl, calling the probe 'shocking'. The Court also condemned the Judicial Magistrate for recording the victim's statement near the accused. The Court ordered the Gurugram Police Commissioner and Investigating Officer to appear in person, highlighting systemic failures in handling child sexual abuse cases and judicial processes.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Monday expressed strong disapproval of the manner in which the Haryana Police handled the investigation into the rape of a three-year-old girl, terming the conduct of the probe "shocking" and insensitive. The Court also took serious note of the complaint that the Judicial Magistrate recorded the statement of the victim in close proximity to the accused persons. The Court directed the Commissioner of Police, Gurugram and the Investigating Officer to remain personally present

articles.title: The Sattankulam custodial torture deaths of a father and son in Tamil Nadu | Explained
articles.summary: In June 2020, during India's COVID-19 lockdown, a father and son died in police custody in Sattankulam, Tamil Nadu. The incident, involving alleged torture by police, shocked the public and led to widespread protests demanding justice and accountability. Civil society groups and activists called for systemic reforms in policing and custodial safety.

articles.title: Hidden Charges That Can Kill Your Tax-Saving Strategy
articles.summary: The article highlights that many tax-saving investment options in India come with hidden costs like Securities Transaction Tax (STT), expense ratios, and exit loads. These charges can significantly reduce the effective returns on investments, despite the tax benefits they offer. The analysis suggests that such costs may warrant regulatory or policy attention to ensure transparency and fairness for investors.
articles.raw_summary: Many tax-saving investments carry hidden costs like STT, expense ratios, and exit loads. The analysis highlights that these charges significantly reduce effective returns despite tax benefits. The post Hidden Charges That Can Kill Your Tax-Saving Strategy appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Mumbai: No U/s 36(1)(iii) Disallowance If Own Funds Exceed Advances
articles.summary: ITAT held that where interest-free funds exceed advances, a presumption arises that such advances are made from own funds. Disallowance under section 36(1)(iii) was deleted as no nexus with borrowed funds was proven. articles.summary: The Tribunal ruled that section 44ADA applies only to specified professions and cannot be invoked for business income covered under section 44AD. Arbitrary substitution of a higher rate by the AO was held unsustainable. articles.title: Denial of police recruitment to person acquitted in Heinous Crime justified on Benefit of Doubt: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that an acquittal based on 'benefit of doubt' does not automatically entitle a candidate to public employment, such as police recruitment. The court held that mere involvement in a heinous crime or moral turpitude can disqualify a person from government jobs. This decision impacts hiring policies for public sector roles in India.
articles.raw_summary: An acquittal based on the benefit of doubt did not confer upon a candidate an automatic right to appointment in public service. Mere involvement of a person in an alleged offence or in the act of moral turpitude may become sufficient enough to apply it as debilitating factor for such candidate to be offered employment. The post Denial of police recruitment to person acquitted in Heinous Crime justified on Benefit of Doubt: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Dalmia Cement to Source 8.1 MW of Wind Power from Ultra Mega Power
articles.summary: Dalmia Cement (Bharat) has agreed to source up to 8.1 MW of wind power from Ventora Energy, a subsidiary of Ultra Mega Power, in Tamil Nadu. The company will acquire a 26.6% stake in Ventora Energy for ₹44.2 million (~$471,066) by purchasing 4.42 million equity shares. This investment supports Dalmia Cement's renewable energy requirements and aligns with India's broader push for clean energy adoption.

Dalmia Cement (Bharat) will acquire 4.42 million equity shares at ₹10 (~$0.11) each, totaling ₹44.2 million (~$471,066), for a 26.6% stake in Ventora Energy. articles.title: ACEN India Secures ₹7.52 Billion for 100 MW Wind Project in Karnataka
articles.summary: ACEN India, a subsidiary of the Philippines-based renewable energy company ACEN Renewables International, has raised ₹7.52 billion (~$80.15 million) from Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group and Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation for its 100-MW wind power project in Bijapur, Karnataka. The company has signed the power purchase agreement (PPA) for the project with SJVN. The project, expected to be commissioned in 2027, will generate around 330 million units of clean energy annually.

articles.title: Stock markets plunge after Trump’s ultimatum on Iran
articles.summary: Global stock markets fell sharply after US President Donald Trump threatened to 'obliterate' Iran’s power plants unless the Strait of Hormuz is opened. The warning caused slumps in Asian and European markets, including Japan’s Nikkei (down 3.4%) and China’s CSI 300 (down 2.8%). Investors are bracing for higher oil prices, which could affect India’s energy imports and inflation.

articles.title: Government orders Tata Power’s Gujarat plant to operate at full output
articles.summary: The Indian government has ordered Tata Power to operate its Gujarat-based power plant at full capacity. This directive comes as the country's peak electricity demand is expected to reach around 270 GW, which is significantly higher than last year. The move aims to address the anticipated surge in power requirements during peak periods.
articles.raw_summary: The move comes as peak demand is expected to touch around 270 GW, significantly higher than last year.

articles.title: Reopening Invalid Because No Failure to Disclose Material Facts: SC Upholds HC Ruling
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld a High Court ruling that tax reassessment beyond four years is invalid if no material facts were undisclosed during the original assessment. The case involved a dispute over whether the Income Tax Department had jurisdiction to reopen a taxpayer's case without evidence of failure to disclose key information. The ruling reinforces legal safeguards against arbitrary tax reassessments and clarifies procedural limits for tax authorities.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether reassessment beyond four years was valid without failure to disclose material facts. The courts held that all relevant details were already disclosed during original assessment, making reopening without jurisdiction. The post Reopening Invalid Because No Failure to Disclose Material Facts: SC Upholds HC Ruling appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Two-day National District Mineral Foundation Summit from Today to boost development in mining-affected districts
articles.summary: The Ministry of Mines is hosting a two-day National District Mineral Foundation (DMF) Summit starting today. The event aims to improve coordination between governments and identify policy reforms to better utilize DMF funds in districts affected by mining, particularly ADP and ABP areas. The goal is to ensure these funds are used effectively and yield tangible outcomes for local development.

articles.title: Terra Clean Invites Bids to Develop a 100 MW Wind Project in Gujarat
articles.summary: Terra Clean, an Indian Oil Corporation (IOCL) subsidiary, has invited bids to develop a 100 MW end-to-end intrastate transmission system (InSTS)-connected wind project in Gujarat. The last date to submit bids is April 17, 2026. Bids will be opened on the same day. Bidders must submit an earnest money deposit (EMD) of ₹23.69 million (~$254,201) and a bidding fee for the electronic tendering system of ₹30,000 (~$321.91).

articles.summary: Farmers in Tiruttani, Tamil Nadu, who recently formed a producer organization to grow millets, are seeking government assistance for machinery to process their crops. Residents also report delays in the construction of a second access road to the Subramaniaswami Temple, with land acquisition still ongoing. The issues reflect local livelihood concerns and infrastructure gaps that may require policy attention.

articles.title: Gold prices down 18% since Iran war! Why yellow metal is not acting as safe haven this time
articles.summary: Gold prices have fallen 18% since the Iran war began, defying its usual role as a safe-haven asset. The decline is driven by a stronger US dollar, rising oil prices, and profit-taking after last year's rally. A hawkish US Federal Reserve, higher bond yields, and liquidity-driven selling have further dampened investor sentiment. The conflict in West Asia and global financial conditions may prompt India to monitor economic risks and adjust policies accordingly.

articles.title: Global Market | Oil shock shatters Fed rate cut bets, sends bond markets into turmoil
articles.summary: Escalating conflict in Iran and rising global oil prices have disrupted bond markets worldwide, leading traders to abandon expectations of U.S. Federal Reserve interest rate cuts. This shift has forced a reassessment of market strategies globally. The developments are tied to geopolitical tensions and their economic ripple effects.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a petition filed by Gitanjali J Angmo, wife of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, challenging his detention under the National Security Act (NSA). articles.title: Anti-bodies not snake venom—why SC quashed case against YouTuber Elvish Yadav
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India quashed a case against YouTuber Elvish Yadav after observing that Noida Police filed a common chargesheet for two separate FIRs and the magistrate took cognisance of it, which was legally invalid. The top court ruled that this procedural error invalidated the entire case. The decision highlights the importance of proper legal procedures in police investigations and court proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: Top court observed that while there were 2 distinct FIRs against Elvish Yadav, Noida Police filed a common chargesheet and the magistrate took cognisance of it, which was invalid.

articles.title: Rupee nears 94-mark vs USD: Middle East tensions drag currency to new low, what's next?
articles.summary: Middle East tensions are significantly impacting global financial markets, pushing the Indian Rupee to record lows near 94 against the US dollar. Analysts believe crude oil prices and geopolitical developments, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz, will dictate the currency's next move. The Reserve Bank of India is expected to intervene to manage volatility.

This decline, attributed to inflation fears and a stronger US dollar, marks the fourth consecutive week of losses for the yellow metal. Analysts anticipate continued downward pressure amid geopolitical tensions and potential interest rate hikes.

articles.title: Asian stocks today: Markets trade in red amid Middle East standoff; Kospi down 6%, Nikkei sheds 1,800 points
articles.summary: Asian stock markets fell sharply, with South Korea's Kospi dropping 6% and Japan's Nikkei losing 1,800 points, amid fears of a worsening energy crisis. Investors sold off technology and consumer stocks while buying energy-linked shares due to rising oil prices and concerns over global supply chains. The sell-off was driven by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz and potential US-Iran conflict.
articles.raw_summary: Asian markets experienced a significant sell-off, with South Korea's Kospi plunging 6%, as fears of a deepening energy crisis gripped investors. Geopolitical tensions, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz and potential US-Iran conflict, fueled the market panic. Investors fled technology and consumption sectors, while energy-linked stocks saw inflows amid concerns over global supply chains and rising oil prices.

articles.title: 'Crisis worse than two 1970s oil shocks put together': IEA chief's big warning on Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned of an unprecedented energy crisis, describing it as worse than the combined oil shocks of the 1970s. The crisis stems from damaged energy infrastructure and threats to the Strait of Hormuz, a vital route for global oil and gas shipments. The IEA emphasizes the need for international cooperation to prevent widespread fuel shortages and stabilize the global economy. The situation could have significant implications for India's energy security and po
articles.raw_summary: The world faces an unprecedented energy crisis, a potent mix of oil and gas shocks, warns the International Energy Agency. With infrastructure damaged and the vital Strait of Hormuz threatened, global economic stability is at risk. The agency stresses the need for international cooperation to resolve the escalating situation and prevent widespread fuel shortages.

articles.title: Vedanta shares plunge 5% as SC rules conglomerate not entitled to concessional rates for high-speed diesel
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that Vedanta is not entitled to concessional rates for high-speed diesel under Form C, as the company allegedly misused the benefit beyond mining purposes. Vedanta's shares fell 5% following the verdict, which adds regulatory pressure amid ongoing legal challenges. The decision affects Vedanta's operational costs and has broader implications for fuel subsidy policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Vedanta shares fell after the Supreme Court upheld a ruling denying concessional diesel rates under Form C, citing misuse beyond mining purposes. The verdict adds regulatory pressure amid ongoing legal challenges and corporate developments, including its move against Adani’s Jaiprakash Associates deal and an upcoming board meeting on dividend consideration.

articles.title: New I-T Act framework notified; taxpayer relief with stricter compliance from April 1
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act has introduced new rules effective April 1, offering relief to taxpayers through increased house rent allowance limits in major cities and expanded employer-provided benefits like food coupons and cars. However, stricter disclosure requirements have been imposed on businesses and professionals, increasing compliance responsibilities for auditors and companies.
articles.raw_summary: New Income Tax Act rules are now in effect from April 1. These rules offer relief to taxpayers with increased house rent allowance limits in major cities. Employer-provided benefits like food coupons and cars are also expanded. Stricter disclosure requirements are introduced for businesses and professionals. Auditors and companies face greater responsibility in this compliance-driven regime.

The party has finalized candidates for all seats.

articles.title: Judiciary Can't Be An Eternal Critic Or A Cheerleader : Justice Ujjal Bhuyan
articles.summary: Justice Ujjal Bhuyan of the Supreme Court stated that the judiciary must maintain its institutional balance and cannot act as a perpetual critic or supporter of the government. He emphasized that the judiciary's constitutional duty is to act as a vigilant sentinel safeguarding foundational principles. Justice Bhuyan remarked, "Judiciary must remain judiciary. Judiciary can neither be an eternal critic, nor a cheerleader. It is the sentinel on the qui vive."

articles.title: CERC Approves Tariffs for NHPC’s 1.2 GW Wind-Solar Hybrid Power Projects
articles.summary: The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC) has approved tariffs of ₹3.41 (~$0.036)/kWh and ₹3.42 (~$0.037)/kWh for NHPC’s 1.2 GW wind-solar hybrid power projects. The approval includes power purchase agreements (PPAs) and ensures alignment with Ministry of Power guidelines. This decision supports India’s renewable energy goals by facilitating large-scale clean power projects.
articles.raw_summary: The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC) has approved tariffs of ₹3.41 (~$0.036)/kWh and ₹3.42 (~$0.037)/kWh for NHPC’s 1.2 GW of interstate transmission system (ISTS)-connected wind-solar hybrid power projects with a greenshoe option. It also approved the power purchase agreements (PPAs) and clarified that the trading margin and implementation structure would ensure alignment with the Ministry of Power guidelines.

articles.title: Why cab drivers across Gurugram are on strike today
articles.summary: Cab drivers in Gurugram are on strike today, demanding the government introduce customer KYC (Know Your Customer) requirements to reduce attacks on drivers. The drivers argue that verifying customers could prevent violent incidents. The strike highlights concerns about driver safety and the need for regulatory intervention.
articles.raw_summary: The drivers are demanding the introduction of customer KYC to reduce the incidents of attacks on drivers.

articles.title: Anil Agarwal's Vedanta moves NCLAT against Adani's bid for Jaypee group's flagship firm
articles.summary: Vedanta has filed an appeal with the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) against Adani's bid for Jaypee Group's flagship firm. Vedanta had previously called the approval a 'commercial conspiracy' and sought reconsideration of its own bid. The dispute highlights competitive tensions in India's corporate sector and may influence future regulatory decisions on mergers and acquisitions.
articles.raw_summary: Vedanta had earlier termed the approval a “commercial conspiracy” and sought reconsideration of its bid.

articles.title: Pakistan raises fuel cost by 200pc amid Strait of Hormuz choke
articles.summary: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif approved a 200% increase in fuel costs for high-octane fuel used by luxury vehicles. This move comes amid tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route. The decision may impact regional fuel prices and energy security considerations for neighboring countries like India.
articles.raw_summary: Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif approved the significant increase in the levy on high-octane fuel used by luxury vehicles.

articles.title: Iran threatens to target power plants across Gulf, UAE's Barakah nuclear plant after Trump's Strait of Hormuz ultimatum
articles.summary: Donald Trump warned Iran via social media that if it did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz to all ships, the US would target Iran’s power plants. Iran responded by threatening to strike power plants across the Gulf, including the UAE’s Barakah nuclear plant. The standoff raises concerns about regional stability and potential disruptions to global oil supplies, which could affect India’s energy imports.
articles.raw_summary: Donald Trump said in a social media post that if Tehran didn't open the Strait of Hormuz to all ships, the US would “obliterate” Iran’s power plants.

articles.title: Saudi Aramco chief to skip key energy event in US as oil crises grows amid Strait of Hormuz tensions
articles.summary: Saudi Aramco's CEO will skip a key energy event in the US as tensions rise in the Strait of Hormuz, forcing the world's largest oil producer to reroute much of its crude. The situation highlights growing oil supply disruptions amid regional conflicts, which could impact global energy markets and India's oil imports. The article underscores the geopolitical risks to energy security that may require policy responses from importing nations like India.
articles.raw_summary: Amid the Hormuz row, Aramco, the world’s largest oil producing company, is facing an energy crisis as it is having to reroute much of its crude.

articles.title: Amid growing war with Iran, US treasury secretary says Trump is ‘defanging Iranian regime’
articles.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated that former President Trump is using strong measures, including threats to destroy Iranian power plants, to counter Iran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz. The statement highlights escalating tensions between the US and Iran, which could impact global oil supplies and regional stability. India, as a major oil importer, may face indirect consequences from such geopolitical developments.
articles.raw_summary: Scott Bessent said that Trump is using ‘the only language the Iranians understand” by threatening to destroy its power plants over Hormuz stalemate.

articles.title: Iran US war LIVE updates: Iran threatens to deploy 'naval mines' in Persian Gulf to teach US a lesson
articles.summary: Iran has threatened to deploy naval mines in the Persian Gulf and attack Middle Eastern electrical plants powering U.S. military bases, escalating tensions with the U.S. Former U.S. President Trump warned that Iranian power plants would be targeted if Tehran did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping within 48 hours. The situation raises concerns about potential disruptions to global oil supplies and regional security.
articles.raw_summary: Iran US war LIVE updates: Iran on Monday threatened to attack Mideast electrical plants powering American military bases. On Saturday, Trump warned that Iranian power plants would be targeted if Tehran failed to "fully open" the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping within 48 hours.

articles.title: Veteran BJP leader Datta Meghe passes away at 89; great loss for Vidarbha, says Gadkari

articles.title: AI Doesn't Have Qualities Of A Judge: CJI Surya Kant

articles.title: Half Of Civil Litigation Can Be Shifted To Mediation In 25 Years With Right Strategies: Sriram Panchu
articles.summary: Legal expert Sriram Panchu proposed strategies to make mediation a more effective and attractive alternative to civil litigation in India. He suggested that with the right approaches, up to half of civil disputes could be resolved through mediation within 25 years. This could reduce court backlogs and improve access to justice. Panchu emphasized the need for systemic changes to promote mediation as a primary dispute resolution tool.

Yet despite its centrality to India’s water security, groundwater has long been poorly understood and weakly governed.

The situation may force India to reassess its energy import strategies and contingency plans.

The government is implementing coordinated policies and infrastructure plans to transform the system. Efforts aim to cut high logistics costs, estimated at nearly 8 percent of GDP. Initiatives like the National Logistics Policy and PM GatiShakti are driving this change.

articles.title: India explores ways for Russia to use surplus rupee reserves, says RBI official
articles.summary: India is evaluating options to help Russia deploy its growing rupee reserves from oil trade, as the RBI considers new investment avenues amid shifting import trends and geopolitical pressures.

articles.title: Sebi board to consider FPI settlement norms ease, intermediary reforms on Monday
articles.summary: Markets regulator Sebi board is set to meet on Monday to deliberate on a wide-ranging agenda, including a proposal to ease fund settlement norms for foreign portfolio investors (FPIs), and changes to regulatory frameworks for market intermediaries, people familiar with the matter said.

articles.title: CJI Surya Kant steps aside from EC appointment case, flags ‘conflict of interest’
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant recused himself from hearing petitions related to the law for appointing Election Commissioners, citing a conflict of interest. The Supreme Court postponed the case hearing to April 7. The case pertains to the legal framework governing the appointment of Election Commissioners in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court postponed hearing petitions on the law for appointing Election Commissioners after CJI Surya Kant recused himself due to a conflict of interest. The case is set for April 7.

articles.title: India’s cotton acreage loses ground to rice, maize amid low productivity
articles.summary: With cotton productivity remaining volatile due to challenges including erratic weather and pest attacks, the country’s area under cotton has fallen by nearly a fifth to 11.2 million hectares in 2025-26 from 13.4 million hectares in 2019-20, according to the latest government data. nbsp;

articles.title: Oil down 2% as US considers lifting sanctions on Iranian oil loaded on vessels
articles.summary: Oil prices dropped over 2% after the US indicated it might ease sanctions on Iranian oil shipments. Global powers also supported efforts to secure the Strait of Hormuz, reducing concerns about oil supply disruptions. The developments could influence India's energy imports and policy decisions related to crude oil sourcing and trade routes.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices fell over 2% as the US signalled potential easing of Iran sanctions and global powers backed efforts to secure Hormuz routes, easing supply concerns after recent spikes.

articles.title: India maps supply chain gaps in pharma, textiles, fertilizers amid West Asia war
articles.summary: A questionnaire circulated by the commerce ministry’s supply chain division to key stakeholders has asked firms to disclose granular data on import dependence, covering specific items, source countries, reliance on foreign suppliers, and whether key inputs are ‘irreplaceable or non-substitutable’.
articles.raw_summary: A questionnaire circulated by the commerce ministry’s supply chain division to key stakeholders has asked firms to disclose granular data on import dependence, covering specific items, source countries, reliance on foreign suppliers, and whether key inputs are ‘irreplaceable or non-substitutable’.

articles.title: Iran’s caused a rates crisis for the world’s central banks. Why the ECB should follow the Fed.
articles.summary: The conflict involving Iran has driven up global oil prices, reversing progress on controlling inflation. Central banks worldwide, including the European Central Bank (ECB), may need to adjust their policies in response. The article suggests that the ECB should follow the U.S. Federal Reserve's approach to managing this economic challenge.
articles.raw_summary: Just when policymakers thought they had finally overcome stubborn inflation, the Iran conflict sparked a rally in oil prices.

articles.title: From Jamnagar to Siberia: How India’s ‘skill capital’ is powering Russia’s war-strained economy
articles.summary: From sub-zero Siberian oil fields to the shadow of drone strikes, Indian workers are ditching West Asia for Russia’s high-stakes war economy. That’s a pursuit of a bigger paycheck.

articles.summary: Corporate tax collections, net of refunds, stood at ₹10.92 trillion, registering a 12.77% year-on-year increase. Non-corporate tax collections, largely comprising personal income tax, came in at ₹11.32 trillion, marking a 2.70% growth.

articles.summary: The conflict in West Asia has disrupted gas and fertilizer supplies, risking a repeat of Ukraine-war-era subsidy pressures and spillover into inflation.
articles.raw_summary: The conflict in West Asia has disrupted gas and fertilizer supplies, risking a repeat of Ukraine-war-era subsidy pressures and spillover into inflation.

articles.title: Oil prices down 1% as US crude inventories rise; supply concerns persist
articles.summary: Crude oil prices fell by 1% due to rising US inventories. However, supply concerns in West Asia and disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz capped losses. This may impact India's oil imports and prices.
articles.raw_summary: Crude prices fell over 1% on rising US inventories, but losses were capped by supply concerns amid the West Asia conflict and disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.summary: The Centre is making QR code-based digital certification mandatory for all retail shops. This will empower consumers to verify compliance with pricing and measurement norms via smartphone. The move aims to help fight overcharging at retail outlets.

articles.title: Buying gold on Gudi Padwa, Ugadi? Don’t skip these purity checks to safeguard your festive purchase

articles.raw_summary: Even though mandatory gold hallmarking has been in place for five years, recent BIS raids on traders - who displayed hallmarking certificates and the BIS logo—have still uncovered lapses in purity.

articles.title: US takes down ‘cybercrime-as-a-service’ botnets that hijacked over 3 million IoT devices globally
articles.summary: The US Justice Department dismantled four major botnets that hijacked over 3 million IoT devices worldwide, using them to launch cyberattacks. These botnets operated as 'cybercrime-as-a-service,' enabling criminals to extort victims. The operation underscores the global scale of IoT-based cyber threats, which pose risks to critical infrastructure and data security. India may need to strengthen its cybersecurity policies and IoT regulations in response to such transnational threats.
articles.raw_summary: The US Justice Department has dismantled four major botnets responsible for significant DDoS attacks that infected millions of IoT devices globally. These botnets, operating on a 'cybercrime-as-a-service' model, were used by cybercriminals to launch attacks and extort victims for payments.

The senators' probe highlights broader debates over corporate consolidation in the AI sector and its potential regulatory implications.

articles.title: Govt amends norms for exporters availing the interest subvention support
articles.summary: The Indian government has amended rules for exporters receiving interest subvention on credit loans, effective January 2. Exporters will lose benefits if their loans become Non-Performing Assets, and early repayments will only qualify for subvention on the actual outstanding period. Digital processes have been introduced to streamline claims. The changes aim to improve efficiency in trade financing and reduce misuse of government support.

Early loan repayment will only receive subvention for the actual outstanding period. Digital processes are introduced for smoother claims. These changes aim to streamline benefits for trade and banks.

articles.title: ICAR–IVRI achieves breakthrough in Sahiwal breeding using advanced reproductive technologies
articles.summary: The technology involves ovum pick-up, in vitro fertilization, and embryo transfer. This opens new avenues for rapid genetic improvement of India's dairy breeds.

articles.title: India-China border trade via Lipulekh Pass to resume after six years
articles.summary: Border trade between India and China via Lipulekh Pass in Pithoragarh is set to resume. Preparations are underway for the trade session, typically from June to September. This move follows government directives and clearances. Traders can retrieve goods stored in Tibet. The resumption aims for better coordination and facilitation.

articles.title: Government amends guidelines for interest subvention support for export credit
articles.summary: The government has amended guidelines for interest subvention support on export credit loans. Under the new rules, interest subvention will no longer be available once a loan account is classified as a non-performing asset (NPA). This change aims to ensure fiscal discipline in export credit financing.

articles.title: Anti dumping duty suggested on spandex, raw material for foam imported from China, Vietnam
articles.summary: India's Directorate General of Trade Remedies has recommended anti-dumping duties for five years on spandex and flexible slabstock polyol imports. These duties target products from China and Vietnam. The recommendations follow investigations into complaints about dumped goods hurting local manufacturers. The proposed duties aim to protect domestic industries from unfair pricing.

articles.title: FM Sitharaman urges tax officials to focus on fairer demand, faster resolution
articles.summary: During a recent conference, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman highlighted the importance of easing tax payment processes for well-meaning citizens. She championed leveraging cutting-edge technology to pinpoint tax evaders. With the upcoming Income Tax Act, effective April 1, 2025, the goal is to transition India into a more tax-friendly environment.

articles.title: New Income Tax Act 2025 to prioritize simplicity and stakeholder engagement, says FM Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman announced the Income Tax Act 2025, which will replace the 1961 Act with a simpler framework effective April 1, 2026. articles.title: Core sector output eased to a three-month low of 2.3% in February
articles.summary: India's core sector growth decelerated to a three-month low of 2.3% in February, impacted by weaker electricity generation and energy industry declines. Cement and steel showed resilience, but refinery products, crude oil, and natural gas contracted. This slowdown signals a broader economic moderation, with forecasts adjusted due to persistent energy price concerns.

articles.title: Government unveils Rs 20000 crore credit guarantee plan for MFIs
articles.summary: The government has announced an ambitious Rs 20,000 crore credit guarantee scheme targeting microfinance institutions, a lifeline for those grappling with funding challenges. This program not only seeks to ease liquidity constraints but also aims to invigorate credit distribution within the sector, specifically helping small and medium NBFC-MFIs.

articles.title: Centre classifies key energy data as national security; mandates real-time reporting from oil and gas firms
articles.summary: India is now treating vital energy data as a national security matter. All oil and gas companies must share detailed operational information. This move aims to strengthen government oversight and improve responses to supply disruptions. Real-time monitoring of energy supplies will help prioritize critical sectors and make better procurement decisions during crises. This ensures India's energy security.

articles.title: India's core sector growth slows down to 2.3 per cent in February 2026
articles.summary: India's core industries saw slower growth in February, reaching 2.3 percent. This slowdown was mainly due to crude oil and petroleum refinery products. Other sectors like coal, natural gas, and electricity also experienced declines. Steel and cement production showed positive growth. This data reflects the industrial activity across eight key sectors.

articles.title: India’s forex reserves fall $7.05 billion to $709.76 billion as of March 13
articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a significant drop. Reserves fell by $7.05 billion to $709.76 billion in the week ending March 13. This follows a previous decline of $11.68 billion. The Reserve Bank of India intervened heavily in currency markets. The central bank sold dollars to support the rupee. Global oil prices and the Iran conflict contributed to this pressure.

The industry warns of cascading effects on earnings and employment, especially for MSMEs. Vigilance against counterfeit products is also urged.

articles.summary: India will reopen the historic Lipulekh Pass for trade with China in June. This route has been closed since 2020. Improved road connectivity is expected to boost participation. Local traders anticipate economic benefits. The reopening follows a period of geopolitical sensitivity regarding the region. Trade will run from June to September.

articles.title: NITI Aayog proposes National Job Skilling Policy to strengthen India’s skilling ecosystem
articles.summary: A working paper, titled Education and Skilling for Employment: From Credentials to Learning Outcome authored by NITI Aayog member Arvind Virmani, said India’s education and skilling landscape faces challenges such as raising the minimum learning outcomes to the benchmarks appropriate for the per capita GDP levels which we are aiming to reach by 2047 and lowering the drop out rates of students between primary and secondary education.

Ongoing conflicts in the Middle East pose additional risks to trade stability. This slowdown could impact India's export-driven economy and trade policies.

articles.summary: In a fervent display of solidarity, farmers have rallied against key components of the proposed India-US trade agreement, fearing that duty-free imports could devastate local agriculture. articles.title: Crude oil prices ease but remain at elevated levels despite Trump, Iran trading barbs

The US has alternately considered escalating military action and winding down efforts, contributing to uncertainty in global oil markets. This volatility impacts India's fuel costs, inflation, and trade dynamics, potentially prompting policy responses.
articles.raw_summary: Brent, the global benchmark, has surged more than 50% since the start of the war, that has now entered its fourth week, with no signs of easing. Prices are also volatile due to the constant flip-flop by Trump, who, before issuing his 48-hour ultimatum, said that the US is considering "winding down" the military efforts against Iran.

Developed by Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL), the machine allows users to refill composite LPG cylinders in minutes with 24/7 self-service access. This initiative aims to improve convenience and accessibility for LPG consumers in the National Capital Region (NCR).
articles.raw_summary: Gurgaon launches Bharatgas Insta LPG ATM by BPCL at Central Park Flower Valley, dispensing composite LPG cylinders in minutes with 24x7 self service access.

articles.title: Artificial Intelligence must strengthen, not override judiciary, says CJI Justice Surya Kant
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Justice Surya Kant stated that Artificial Intelligence (AI) should support the judiciary by managing large data volumes, identifying patterns, and reducing delays. However, he emphasized that AI must not replace the core judicial function of delivering judgments. The remarks were made in the context of leveraging technology to improve efficiency in India's legal system.
articles.raw_summary: ”AI should assist in handling large volumes of data and records, identifying patterns, and reducing procedural delays. However, it must not encroach upon the core judicial function of delivering judgments,” the CJI was quoted as saying.

articles.title: LPG update | Centre to add 20% more supply to states from Mar 23; hotels, restaurants to be allocated more
articles.summary: The Indian government will increase LPG supply to states by 20% starting March 23, 2026, with additional allocations for hotels, restaurants, dhabas, and other commercial and industrial sectors. The decision aims to address supply needs in these sectors, though the exact rationale for the timing or demand drivers is not specified in the report.
articles.raw_summary: The additional supply will be mainly for restaurants, dhabas, hotels and other commercial and industrial sectors. The additional allocation will come into effect from March 23, 2026, and will continue until further notice.

articles.title: Food prices set to increase as hotels shift to firewood, others; biryani takes the hit during Eid
articles.summary: LPG crisis in Tamil Nadu pushes hotels to firewood, raising biryani prices, hurting Ramzan gatherings and cutting Chennai hotel business by 30 percent.
articles.raw_summary: LPG crisis in Tamil Nadu pushes hotels to firewood, raising biryani prices, hurting Ramzan gatherings and cutting Chennai hotel business by 30 percent.

articles.title: BJP names 9 candidates as AINRC alliance finalises Puducherry seat deal
articles.summary: The BJP has announced nine candidates for the April 9 Puducherry Assembly elections after finalising a seat-sharing pact with AINRC, while AIADMK and LJK have been allotted two seats each in the alliance.

articles.title: Fishermen spot PSLV rocket part off Tamil Nadu coast, ISRO confirms debris
articles.summary: Fishermen off the Tamil Nadu coast spotted and recovered debris from an ISRO PSLV rocket. ISRO confirmed the debris originated from one of its rockets, prompting an investigation into its origin and safety implications. The incident raises questions about space debris management and regulatory oversight in India's space activities.
articles.raw_summary: Debris from an ISRO PSLV rocket has been recovered off Tamil Nadu’s coast after fishermen spotted a floating metallic structure, prompting an investigation into its origin and safety implications.

articles.title: DarkSword spyware puts 270 million iPhones at risk, researchers warn
articles.summary: Researchers have discovered a spyware tool called DarkSword that exploits vulnerabilities in Apple's Safari browser to target iPhones. The malware can expose user data within seconds, putting up to 270 million devices at risk if not updated. Experts warn that this poses a major cybersecurity threat, particularly for users in India where smartphone penetration is high. The incident highlights the need for stronger digital security measures and user awareness.
articles.raw_summary: A newly discovered spyware tool, DarkSword, is targeting iPhones through Safari exploits, exposing data within seconds. Experts warn that up to 270 million devices may be at risk if not updated.

articles.title: DPDP Rules | How to achieve real compliance when intent isn't enough
articles.summary: India's Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Rules have come into force, shifting compliance focus from declarations to verifiable actions. Enterprises now face the challenge of proving compliance rather than just stating intent. The article outlines ten steps to help businesses navigate these new requirements, emphasizing the need for robust documentation and evidence-based adherence to the rules.
articles.raw_summary: With the DPDP Rules now in force, compliance is no longer about what you say—it’s about what you can prove. For enterprises, the real challenge might just have begun. These ten steps can help.

articles.title: 6G spectrum decision likely after standards finalised: Jyotiraditya Scindia
articles.summary: Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia said the ITU and 3GPP will decide on 6G spectrum after standards are set. India aims to help set these standards via Bharat 6G Alliance.
articles.raw_summary: Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia said the ITU and 3GPP will decide on 6G spectrum after standards are set. India aims to help set these standards via Bharat 6G Alliance.

articles.title: RBI wins Initiative of the Year award for domain rollout aimed at fighting cyberfraud
articles.summary: Launched in February 2025, the RBI has mandated banks to shift to a common ‘.bank.in’ domain that is restricted to regulated entities. The idea is to make it easier for users to identify authentic banking platforms and cut down on fraud linked to spoofed websites.

articles.title: Memory chip shortage to continue until 2030, says SK Group chairman
articles.summary: SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won predicts a global shortage of memory chips will persist for the next 4-5 years, with supply unable to meet demand until at least 2030. The shortage is driven by dominant players like SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron struggling to keep up with demand. This could impact industries reliant on semiconductors, including India's growing electronics and tech sectors. The situation may prompt policy measures to address supply chain vulnerabilities or boost domestic production
articles.raw_summary: Chey Tae-won, SK Group chairman, predicts a global memory chip shortage for 4-5 more years. SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron control supply but can't meet demand until 2030.

articles.title: Gold and silver prices in India: What caused the recent volatility and rebound
articles.summary: Gold and silver rose in Friday's futures trade. April gold increased ₹1,914 to ₹1.46 lakh per 10g on MCX, and May silver jumped ₹8,540 to ₹2.40 lakh/kg. Analysts cite easing geopolitical concerns and possible US sanctions relief on Iranian oil.

articles.title: IRDAI seeks ‘significantly’ lower commissions on insurance products listed on Bima Sugam
articles.summary: The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) has urged insurers to significantly lower commissions on insurance products listed on the Bima Sugam platform. Insurers have agreed to offer zero-commission policies with only a nominal fee. Motor insurance products are set to launch next quarter, while health and term life insurance products are expected by FY27.
articles.raw_summary: IRDAI urges lower commissions on Bima Sugam policies. Insurers agree to "zero commission" policies with a nominal fee. Motor insurance launches next quarter; health and term life by FY27.

articles.summary: Net inflows into commodities fell sharply in February, dropping to ₹73,842 crore from ₹1.64 lakh crore in January. Equity inflows declined 19% to ₹42,017 crore.

articles.title: Russia barrels back to top India's oil game as Middle East war shakes markets

articles.title: Explained: The benefits of DGCA's new flight ticket refund rules
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has introduced new rules for flight ticket refunds and amendments. Passengers can now cancel or amend tickets without charges within 48 hours of booking, and correct passenger names without fees within 24 hours. These changes aim to enhance consumer protection in India's aviation sector.
articles.raw_summary: There's now a 48-hour look-in period for flight ticket cancellation or amendment with no charges, and a 24-hour window for name correction without charges.

articles.title: NIOS finances under scrutiny as govt plans to enrol out-of-school children
articles.summary: A recent working paper by the National Institute of Public Finance and Policy (NIPFP), a finance ministry think tank, highlights that NIOS has not received government budget support since 2012–13. Instead, it operates largely as a self-financing body, relying heavily on student fees, the paper notes.

articles.title: Taiwan detects 7 Chinese vessels, 3 ships in its territorial waters, twice in 24 hrs
articles.summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected seven Chinese naval vessels and three official ships operating within its territorial waters twice within 24 hours. This incident highlights ongoing tensions in the Taiwan Strait and may influence India's diplomatic and security assessments regarding China's regional military activities.
articles.raw_summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected the presence of 7 Chinese naval vessels and 3 official ships operating around its territorial waters on Monday.

articles.title: Martyrs’ Day: How India honours soldiers who gave their lives for the nation
EPFO reaches out to subscribers with unclaimed balance
articles.summary: The Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) is creating an automated system to return unclaimed money from inactive accounts directly to subscribers. Initially, this will cover Aadhaar-verified accounts with balances of Rs 1,000 or less, simplifying the withdrawal process for members. The initiative aims to clear idle funds and improve ease of access to entitled benefits.
articles.raw_summary: EPFO is developing an auto-settlement facility to disburse unclaimed money from inoperative accounts directly to subscribers. This initiative will initially target Aadhaar-verified accounts with balances of Rs 1,000 or less, aiming to clear idle funds and simplify the withdrawal process for members.

articles.title: With 7th regiment, Army expands Pinaka fleet
articles.summary: The Indian Army has inducted a 7th regiment equipped with Pinaka multi-barrel rocket launchers, expanding its fleet of this indigenously developed artillery system. The Pinaka is a key part of India's artillery modernization efforts, enhancing long-range strike capabilities. This move strengthens the Army's firepower and aligns with ongoing efforts to bolster indigenous defence production under the 'Make in India' initiative.
articles.raw_summary: With 7th regiment, Army expands Pinaka fleet

articles.title: CAFE 3.0 rollout: Penalty recovery for carmakers remains a grey area
articles.summary: Government seeks clarity on recovering hefty penalties from carmakers for missing fuel efficiency targets. With stricter norms ahead, the PMO is pushing ministries to define who evaluates, approves, and recovers the dues. Over Rs 8,800 crore in penalties have been levied for past non-compliance, yet no recovery has occurred.
articles.raw_summary: Government seeks clarity on recovering hefty penalties from carmakers for missing fuel efficiency targets. With stricter norms ahead, the PMO is pushing ministries to define who evaluates, approves, and recovers the dues. Over Rs 8,800 crore in penalties have been levied for past non-compliance, yet no recovery has occurred.

articles.title: Kilometrage allowance hiked by 25% for train crew
articles.summary: Indian Railways has boosted Kilometrage Allowance and Allowance in lieu of Kilometrage by 25% for over two lakh running staff, effective January 2024. This decision aligns compensation with the 50% Dearness Allowance hike, addressing long-standing demands for better financial benefits for essential operational personnel.
articles.raw_summary: Indian Railways has boosted Kilometrage Allowance and Allowance in lieu of Kilometrage by 25% for over two lakh running staff, effective January 2024. This decision aligns compensation with the 50% Dearness Allowance hike, addressing long-standing demands for better financial benefits for essential operational personnel.

articles.title: Maoist endgame nears, but no political fanfare in offing
articles.summary: The March 31 deadline for eradicating Left-wing extremism in India may pass without official celebration, as security operations have led to the neutralization or surrender of most Maoist leaders. This has fractured the CPI(Maoist) group, pushing remaining members toward surrender. The psychological pressure from the deadline has accelerated the group's decline, though no formal political announcement is expected. The focus remains on sustaining these gains without fanfare.
articles.raw_summary: The March 31 deadline for eliminating Left-wing extremism may pass without fanfare, having successfully created a psychological impetus for stakeholders. This pressure led to the neutralization or surrender of most CPI(Maoist) leadership, fracturing the group and pushing remaining members towards surrender.

articles.title: UAPA misuse not a model for Viksit Bharat: SC judge
articles.summary: SC judge Ujjal Bhuyan emphasized that a developed India by 2047 requires greater tolerance for debate and dissent, without criminalizing them. He highlighted that thousands arrested under the UAPA with minuscule convictions indicate overuse or misuse of the law, hindering justice delivery and the ideal of a developed nation.
articles.raw_summary: SC judge Ujjal Bhuyan emphasized that a developed India by 2047 requires greater tolerance for debate and dissent, without criminalizing them. He highlighted that thousands arrested under the UAPA with minuscule convictions indicate overuse or misuse of the law, hindering justice delivery and the ideal of a developed nation.

articles.title: PM Modi is India's longest serving head of govt
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has become the longest-serving head of government, surpassing the record previously held by Pawan Kumar Chamling. This milestone marks 25 continuous years in leadership, beginning with his tenure as Gujarat Chief Minister in October 2001 and continuing as Prime Minister since May 2014.

articles.title: Energy crisis: Govt pushes for PNG connections, asks all ministries to assess demand
articles.summary: The Indian government has directed all ministries to assess the demand for piped natural gas (PNG) connections as part of efforts to address the ongoing energy crisis. This move aims to expand PNG infrastructure and reduce reliance on other fuels. The assessment will help shape future energy policies and infrastructure development in the country.

articles.title: Kotak sells stake in capital market lending arm
articles.summary: Kotak Mahindra Capital Company will sell nearly 31% of its stake in Infina Finance for approximately Rs 1,293.9 crore. The stake will be acquired by investors including the Rakesh Jhunjhunwala estate and other entities. This transaction will reduce KMCC's holding in the capital market lending arm to 19%.

articles.title: If America strikes us, we should attack Indian cities like Delhi and Mumbai: Ex-Pak envoy
articles.summary: Former Pakistani envoy Abdul Basit stated that striking Indian cities like New Delhi and Mumbai would be Islamabad's "default move" if Pakistan were attacked by the US. He asserted that Pakistan's nuclear deterrence is India-specific, not aimed at the US, and that any aggression would be met with retaliation against India.

articles.title: Back in Ladakh to rousing reception, Wangchuk calls for ‘give and take’ solution
articles.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk returned to Ladakh and urged the Union government to engage in 'win-win' talks with Ladakhi leaders, emphasizing a 'give and take' approach. He reiterated his commitment to continuing the struggle for Ladakh's culture and environment if needed. Wangchuk also sought justice for the September 24 firing incident and the release of detained individuals.
articles.raw_summary: Released activist Sonam Wangchuk urged the Union government for "win-win" talks with Ladakhi leaders, emphasizing a "give and take" approach. He vowed to continue his struggle if necessary, highlighting his fight for Ladakh's culture and environment. Wangchuk expressed hope for justice regarding the September 24 firing and the release of arrested individuals.

articles.title: DGTR recommends anti-dumping duties on Chinese yarn amid ethyl chloroformate probe
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR) has recommended imposing anti-dumping duties on Chinese yarn following an investigation into the use of ethyl chloroformate. This decision aims to protect domestic industries from unfair trade practices. The recommendation is part of India's trade policy framework to address alleged dumping and safeguard local manufacturers.

articles.title: 3 killed, two children injured in bus–car crash on Pune–Nashik highway
articles.summary: A speeding car crossed the divider on a bridge slope and collided with an oncoming bus on the Pune-Nashik highway, killing three people and injuring two children. Police attributed the crash to the car's speed and alleged negligence. The incident raises concerns about road safety and infrastructure on major highways, which may prompt authorities to review traffic regulations or bridge designs.

articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) announced 23 candidates for the upcoming Assam Assembly elections, while the Raijor Dal revised its list of nominees after finalizing a seat-sharing agreement. The elections are scheduled for April 9. This development reflects political maneuvering ahead of the polls, which could impact future policy directions in the state.
articles.raw_summary: TMC releases consolidated list of 23 candidates, Raijor Dal revises nominees after seat-sharing deal ahead of Assam Assembly elections on April 9.

articles.title: AI can aid judiciary but not replace judges: SC Justice Vikram Nath
articles.summary: Supreme Court Justice Vikram Nath stated that artificial intelligence (AI) can assist courts but cannot replace judges, emphasizing the importance of human judgment, ethics, and nuance in judicial decision-making. The remarks highlight the need for careful integration of AI in the legal system while preserving the role of human oversight.
articles.raw_summary: Justice Vikram Nath says AI can aid courts but cannot replace judges, stressing human judgment, ethics and nuance in judicial decision-making.

articles.title: KC Tyagi joins Jayant Choudhary's RLD days after quitting JD(U)
articles.summary: KC Tyagi, a founding member of Nitish Kumar's Janata Dal (United) party, resigned on March 17 without specifying a reason. Days later, he joined Jayant Choudhary's Rashtriya Lok Dal. This political shift could influence future policy directions or alliances in Indian politics.
articles.raw_summary: KC Tyagi, a founding member of Nitish Kumar's party, resigned from the party on March 17. He did not specify any reason behind his exit from JD(U).

articles.title: Ropeway trolley returning from temple falls in Chhattisgarh; 1 dead, 6 injured
articles.summary: A ropeway trolley returning from Khallari Mata temple in Chhattisgarh’s Mahasamund snapped, killing one woman and injuring six others during the Navratri festival. The accident raises concerns about the safety of ropeway infrastructure, particularly in tourist or religious sites. Authorities may need to review existing regulations or implement stricter safety protocols to prevent similar incidents.
articles.raw_summary: Ropeway cable snaps near Khallari Mata temple in Chhattisgarh’s Mahasamund, killing a woman and injuring six during Navratri visit.

articles.title: Manipur: ‘Ice-breaking’ meet marks first Manipur peace outreach in 3 years
articles.summary: A meeting was held in Guwahati on Saturday between Manipur's government and representatives, marking the first engagement since violent clashes broke out in May 2023. The violence has caused significant unrest, prompting this outreach effort. The meeting aims to address the ongoing conflict and restore peace in the state. No direct policy decisions were announced.
articles.raw_summary: The meeting was held in Guwahati on Saturday and marked the first engagement between the two sides since violence broke out on May 3, 2023.

articles.title: ‘Some people only want to do politics’: Fadnavis slams Opposition amid row over ‘godman’ Ashok Kharat's arrest
articles.summary: Nashik police arrested Ashok Kharat, a self-styled godman and numerologist, for allegedly raping a woman who sought his astrological advice. Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis criticized the Opposition, accusing them of politicizing the issue. The case raises concerns about the regulation of religious practitioners and women's safety, potentially prompting calls for policy or legal reforms.
articles.raw_summary: Ashok Kharat, a self-styled godman and numerologist, was arrested by Nashik police for allegedly raping a woman who had approached him for astrological advice.

articles.title: DICGC risk-based premium: How much will banks pay for deposit insurance?
articles.summary: The Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC) has introduced a risk-based premium (RBP) framework for banks, offering discounts of up to 33.33% on deposit insurance premiums based on a bank's rating category. Banks may also receive an additional 25% incentive for long-term stability (vintage). This move aims to align insurance costs with risk levels, potentially reducing financial burdens for well-rated banks while ensuring stronger regulatory oversight of the banking sector.
articles.raw_summary: RBP framework provides a bank with a discount of up to 33.33% on the deposit insurance premium based on its rating category and an up to 25% vintage incentive.

articles.title: Limits in health insurance: Why is it important to check them?
articles.summary: Most health insurance plans in India impose sub-limits on specific medical procedures, modern treatments, and pre- and post-hospitalization expenses. These limits can restrict coverage for policyholders, raising concerns about transparency and consumer protection. The article highlights why checking these sub-limits is important for individuals purchasing health insurance. No direct quotes are included.
articles.raw_summary: Most health plans have sub-limits on some features for specified medical procedures, modern treatments, and pre- and post-hospitalisation expenses, etc.

articles.title: Iranian Missile Strikes Are Costing Big Oil Billions in Lost Revenue
articles.summary: Iranian missile strikes have severely damaged energy infrastructure in the region, disrupting oil production and exports. This is expected to take years to recover, leading to billions in lost revenue for major oil companies. The incident raises concerns about global oil supply stability and potential price volatility, which could impact India's energy imports and economic policies.
articles.raw_summary: Damaged energy infrastructure is likely to take years to come back online

articles.title: US‑Iran war: Could US troops enter Iran’s Kharg Island? Bessent says ‘all options are on the table’
articles.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated that all military options, including deploying troops to Iran's Kharg Island, are under consideration amid escalating US-Iran tensions. The Kharg Island is a key oil terminal in Iran. This development could impact global oil markets and regional security, which are critical for India's energy imports and foreign policy decisions.
articles.raw_summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent indicated that military deployment to Iran's Kharg Island is a possibility amid rising tensions.

articles.title: Mind The Gap: Identity under scrutiny
articles.summary: An amendment has been introduced that fundamentally changes how identity is recognized for transgender individuals in India, marking a major shift in transgender rights. This move is seen as a significant upset to existing protections and could have wide-ranging implications for legal recognition and social inclusion. The amendment reflects a policy action rather than a demand or discussion phase.
articles.raw_summary: In one of the biggest upsets to transgender rights, an amendment makes a fundamental shift in how identity is recognised.

articles.summary: Justice Dipankar Datta made candid remarks about the collegium system, which appoints judges to higher courts in India. The remarks highlight issues within the system and call for reflection on its functioning. The collegium system is a key governance mechanism for judicial appointments, and its reform could have significant legal and administrative implications.

articles.summary: An editorial in Hindustan Times calls for Tel Aviv and Washington to end the war in West Asia. It argues that the ongoing conflict has global repercussions and urges diplomatic intervention to establish a truce. The piece highlights the need for international pressure to address the humanitarian and geopolitical crisis.

articles.title: Why we are thirsty on a blue planet
articles.summary: This article by Aun Abdullah, Programme Director of Sustainable Urbanisation at Lodha Foundation, examines India's water scarcity crisis. It explores the challenges of water management in a rapidly urbanizing and climate-vulnerable country. The piece underscores the need for sustainable solutions to address the growing demand for water resources.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Aun Abdullah, programme director, Sustainable Urbanisation, Lodha Foundation.

articles.title: Turning a global policy shift into a clean energy advantage
articles.summary: The article, authored by Upendra Tripathy and Jagjeet Sareen, explores how a global shift in clean energy policies could be leveraged as an advantage for India. It highlights the need for India to align with international trends to boost its energy transition and reduce reliance on fossil fuels. The authors emphasize the strategic importance of adopting clean energy policies to enhance India's economic and environmental goals.

articles.title: The 10-Day Countdown: Qatar Strikes And The Impending LNG Collapse
articles.summary: Qatar, which supplies 20% of the world's liquefied natural gas (LNG), halted exports after Iran blockaded the Strait of Hormuz at the start of a conflict. This disruption threatens global LNG supply chains, including India's energy imports. The incident underscores vulnerabilities in energy trade routes critical to India's fuel security.
articles.raw_summary: Qatar, which produces a fifth of the world's liquefied natural gas, had to stop exports after Iran blockaded the Strait of Hormuz at the mouth of the Gulf, in the first few days of the conflict.

articles.title: Video: Priests Drag, Punch Devotees Inside Temple In Kolhapur
articles.summary: A video surfaced showing priests at Maharashtra's Jyotiba Temple in Kolhapur assaulting devotees during morning rituals. The incident contradicts the usual perception of temples as places of peace. The video has sparked outrage and may lead to calls for stricter regulations or oversight of religious institutions in India.
articles.raw_summary: People usually visit temples for peace and calm, but a tense scene unfolded at Maharashtra's Jyotiba Temple in Kolhapur when a video emerged showing priests assaulting devotees during morning rituals.
